channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Kuwait closes airspace, Israel warns of launches from Lebanon after U.S strikes in Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump promised more attacks on Iran after strikes resumed this week. Earlier this week, he said a deal to end the war was near.

item.title: Oracle beats on earnings, but stock drops on plans to raise another $20 billion
item.description: Oracle topped quarterly expectations, but free cash flow is negative, and the company plans to raise more capital for data center projects.

item.title: Stock futures rise as U.S. completes strikes against Iran: Live updates
item.description: A rout in chip stocks and ramp-up in Middle East tensions pulled down the major averages on Wednesday.

item.title: Investors brace for a 'long grind' as Iran war escalation dims hopes of an early end to hostilities
item.description: As U.S. and Iranian forces continue exchanging strikes, investors are increasingly grappling with the possibility that the conflict could become more prolonged.

item.title: These stocks pay attractive dividends and offer 'turmoil insurance'
item.description: Investors looking for stability in times of volatility often turn to dividend stocks — including real estate investment trusts. Here is one analyst's top picks.

item.title: Hugo Boss pops 7% after top shareholder Frasers launches $2 billion takeover offer
item.description: British retail group Frasers owns a roughly 26% stake in Hugo Boss.

item.title: Analysis: Trump said he loves inflation. Why that should be music to Kevin Warsh's ears
item.description: The president said Wednesday he is happy with inflation at 4.2%. That will be welcome news for new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh.

item.title: OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly mulling drastic price cuts to its AI models, as it looks to woo consumers from rival AI company Anthropic, the WSJ reported on Wednesday.

item.title: Oil climbs as U.S.-Iran conflict escalates and regional tensions mount
item.description: Oil prices edged higher on Thursday after Kuwait closed its airspace and Israel warned of launches from Lebanon.

item.title: United Auto Workers reaches deal with Dauch Corp after 10-day strike
item.description: This comes after a 10-day strike against the company at its Three Rivers, Michigan plant

item.title: College sticker prices top $100,000 at 16 schools — but many students pay significantly less
item.description: The cost of attendance at a growing number of colleges is now more than six figures a year, according to data from The Princeton Review.

item.title: World Cup travel boost hasn't materialized for U.S. businesses — yet
item.description: The expected travel boom from the World Cup is looking like it will be a city-by-city, match-by-match test of pricing power.

item.title: Jim Cramer says one of SpaceX's biggest risks is this group of investors
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said one of the biggest risks facing the SpaceX IPO is speculators who may rush to sell shares shortly after the stock begins trading.

item.title: An unusual derivative play off SpaceX’s IPO success: A maker of space gases
item.description: Rothschild & Co. Redburn sees industrial gas supplier Linde emerging as a potential beneficiary alongside SpaceX.

item.title: Trump keeps saying an Iran deal is close. Markets keep believing it
item.description: Analysts still assume that, despite the continued conflict, a deal will eventually be reached that ends the war and reopens the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Inside India newsletter: Hollywood is debating AI. India's filmmakers are embracing it
item.description: Even as Hollywood remains the world's most influential storyteller, experiments in AI filmmaking are increasingly happening in India.

item.title: USDA's Rollins called screwworm a 'little pest' amid U.S. spread. Last year, she called it 'terrifying'
item.description: The U.S. agriculture secretary is downplaying the threat posed by the spreading screwworm now compared with her more dire remarks last year.

item.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says U.S. will attack Iran 'very hard'
item.description: Oil prices rose after the U.S. launched military strikes against Iran, raising concerns that renewed clashes may threaten shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Wall Street needs a crash course in the token economy ahead of AI IPOs. SpaceX offers a preview
item.description: When OpenAI and Anthropic make their IPO prospectuses available to the public, investors are going to have to learn about a whole new economy.

item.title: Trump says U.S. secretly moved more than 100 million barrels of oil through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump's comments come after Energy Secretary Chris Wright said Tuesday that oil exports through Hormuz are "rising very meaningfully."

item.title: Starbucks stock is a bright spot in Wednesday's bleak market. Here's why
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Chase unveils massive Sapphire Preferred overhaul: Enhanced rewards, new perks and a crushing transfer partner change
item.description: The Chase Sapphire Preferred is getting a big update. Here's what you need to know about the good and the bad changes happening soon.

item.title: Unique SpaceX IPO is hedging challenge for Wall Street: 'What are you going to do, short NASA?'
item.description: Millbank Dartmoor Portsmouth CIO Dennis Davitt says he hasn't seen anything like the SpaceX IPO since 2004. Investors are nervous about their positions.

item.title: Bisignano says Social Security Administration's phone helpline wait times have reached a record low
item.description: Long waits when calling the Social Security Administration's 800 number have been a legacy issue. Commissioner Frank Bisignano said he's changing that.

item.title: Company moves from Singapore to cheaper, more spacious Malaysia show rising global mobility trend
item.description: Lower costs, tax incentives and access to a larger market have seen a raft of companies shift operations to Malaysia from Singapore.

item.title: Volatility surge has trader eyeing one 'stable' stock
item.description: Mike Khouw is looking for a stable, cash-generating business where he can sell volatility instead of buying drama.

item.title: Citigroup shares outperform down market after Trump endorsement
item.description: Jane Fraser-led Citi fell 1%, less than the rest of the market and some other major banks, after Trump lauded its turnaround in a social media post.

item.title: As gold's tumble continues, traders bet the pain may last for two more years
item.description: Selling in the precious metal just keeps getting worse, with the GLD ETF now down 25% from its intraday record in February.

item.title: Commercial real estate saw record lending competition in April, according to JLL
item.description: Global credit activity among lenders as well as overall competitiveness of loan terms reached an all-time high in April, JLL found.

item.title: Sen. Warren calls on SEC to delay SpaceX IPO, flagging concerns about valuation and governance
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren noted Elon Musk's "uniquely unchecked" power as SpaceX's majority shareholder and other concerns in a letter to the SEC.
